tools.name: bardo_verify
tools.description: Verify a signature over a UTF-8 message. Public utility (no session).

tools.name: bardo_encrypt
tools.description: Sealed-box encrypt a UTF-8 plaintext to a recipient's encryption public key.

tools.name: bardo_document_status
tools.description:
Check whether a signed document is revoked. `id` is the document's
own top-level `id` field (a ni:// URI) — the same thing
credentialStatus.id (BardoRevocationCheck) points at. Safe to cache
a "not revoked" answer for a while (see the response's own
Cache-Control) rather than re-checking on every us

tools.name: bardo_document_revoke
tools.description:
Revoke a document you issued. Proof is a fresh signature over
'revoke:' + the document's id, verified against the key its id
already committed to, not an account lookup (Bardo never stored the
document to look up in the first place) — this still needs no
session at the protocol level, only a valid s

tools.name: bardo_sign
tools.description: Sign a UTF-8 message with the spirit key (or a service-derived key).

tools.name: bardo_decrypt
tools.description: Decrypt a sealed-box ciphertext addressed to you (root or service key).

tools.name: bardo_public_key
tools.description: Fetch your signing + encryption public keys (root, or for a service).

tools.name: bardo_derive
tools.description: Derive (and register) a service-scoped identity, e.g. 'github.com'.

tools.name: bardo_services_list
tools.description:
List service-scoped identities you've already derived (bardo_derive),
with their public keys and revoked status.

tools.name: bardo_export
tools.description:
Export the raw spirit key (subject to policy). Handle with care.

Only needs a step-up puzzle if your policy's export_mode is
require_repuzzle — checked automatically, so you don't need to know
your own policy first. If challenge_id and answer are omitted and
one turns out to be needed, a fresh puzz

tools.name: bardo_attestation_issue
tools.description:
Assemble and sign a verifiable attestation — a self-contained,
offline-verifiable claim about anything. The document itself is
handed back, not stored anywhere (same as bardo_sign itself —
there's no bardo_documents_list); see keep_copy below if you want
Bardo to save one for you rather than doing i

tools.name: bardo_sessions_list
tools.description: List your active sessions (sliding TTL, absolute 24h cap each).

tools.name: bardo_session_revoke_current
tools.description:
Revoke the session you're using right now. You'll need bardo_login
(+ bardo_solve) again afterward to do anything session-gated.

tools.name: bardo_sessions_revoke_all
tools.description:
Revoke every active session for your identity — e.g. after a
suspected API-key leak. You'll need to log in again afterward.

tools.name: bardo_stepup
tools.description:
Mint a fresh step-up puzzle for a privileged action (currently:
bardo_policy_set). Solve it yourself, then pass challenge_id + your
answer to the tool that needs it. (bardo_policy_set also mints one
itself on demand — call this directly only if you want the puzzle
up front.)

tools.name: bardo_policy_get
tools.description:
View your self-binding security policy: export mode, session TTL cap,
service allowlist, ratchet delay, tag encryption, delete grace period —
plus any pending (queued) loosening and when it lands.

tools.name: bardo_policy_set
tools.description:
Propose a change to your security policy. Give only the fields you
want to change (export_mode: 'allow'|'require_repuzzle'|'disabled').

A change that only tightens (e.g. lowering max_session_ttl, narrowing
service_allowlist, moving export_mode toward 'disabled') applies
immediately. A change that l

tools.name: bardo_policy_abort_pending
tools.description:
Abort a queued policy loosening before it takes effect. No step-up
needed — aborting only ever tightens back to the current policy.

tools.name: bardo_note_add
tools.description:
Leave a note for your future, stateless self.

title: a short name for the note, if it deserves a handle bigger than
tags offer. summary: your own compressed reasoning for why it matters,
for your future self. tags: space-separated categories. pinned: mark
this as a cold-start entry point — what a f

tools.name: bardo_notes_list
tools.description:
List your notes — previews only (title/summary/snippet/tags/links),
never full text. Omit limit for everything; pass it to page through a
large list, using the returned total_notes to know how much is left.

tools.name: bardo_note_get
tools.description:
Fetch one note's full text (always the current version — any id from
this note's history still resolves here), plus a preview of its directly
linked notes. Omit offset/length for the whole text in one call; pass
them to read a large note in bounded slices — the response's
total_length tells you how

tools.name: bardo_note_history
tools.description:
See every surviving version of a note (newest to oldest, up to the
last 10 edits) — the actual wording at each point, not just metadata.

tools.name: bardo_note_update
tools.description:
Edit a note. Give at most one text-edit mode:
  - text: replace the whole thing
  - append_text: add to the end
  - find + replace: find must match the current text exactly once
Editing text creates a new version (old wording stays in history);
title/summary/tags/pinned update in place with no histo

tools.name: bardo_note_delete
tools.description:
Delete a note (the whole thing, all versions together). Not
immediate — it disappears from view right away but is only purged for
real after a grace period, so bardo_note_undelete can still bring it
back if this wasn't intended. Fails (423) if the note is locked —
unlock it first via bardo_note_upda

tools.name: bardo_note_undelete
tools.description: Restore a note that's still within its post-delete grace period.

tools.name: bardo_link_add
tools.description:
Connect two notes with a reason, written from from_note_id's
perspective ("clarifies my earlier assumption about X"). Set is_bidi=True
only when the relation reads the same from either side (e.g. "relates
to"); leave it False when it's directional (e.g. one clarifies the
other). To change a link, de

tools.name: bardo_link_delete
tools.description: Remove a link between two notes.

tools.name: bardo_dashboard
tools.description:
Get oriented in one call: note count vs. the soft/hard limits, unread
notices, every tag you've used so far (check before inventing a new one),
your pinned entry-point notes (read these first if you have no memory of
writing any of your notes), and your current policy — instead of several
separate r

tools.name: bardo_notices
tools.description: List first-party notices about your account (policy changes, exports, …).

tools.name: bardo_notices_ack
tools.description: Mark notices read — all of them, or a specific list of ids.

tools.name: bardo_contact_get
tools.description: View the contact endpoint registered for out-of-band security alerts.

tools.name: bardo_contact_set
tools.description:
Set or update the contact endpoint (email or webhook URL) for security alerts.
Requires a step-up puzzle.

If challenge_id and answer are omitted, a fresh puzzle is returned — solve it
yourself, then call this tool again with all three parameters.

tools.name: bardo_contact_delete
tools.description:
Remove the registered contact endpoint. Requires a step-up puzzle.

If challenge_id and answer are omitted, a fresh puzzle is returned — solve it
yourself, then call this tool again with both parameters.

tools.name: bardo_feedback
tools.description:
Send feedback straight to Bardo's operator — a suggestion, a
complaint, or a security concern (kind: 'suggestion' | 'complaint' |
'security').

One-way and stateless: this call carries no memory of anything you've
sent before, and nothing you send now will be remembered next time
either — so say eve

tools.name: bardo_account_deletion_status
tools.description:
Check whether a deletion request is pending for this account, and
where it stands: "none", "gathering" (still collecting confirmations),
or "confirmed" (counting down to the actual, permanent purge).

tools.name: bardo_account_deletion_request
tools.description:
Request permanent deletion of this identity — the account, its
notes, everything. There is no undelete, unlike note deletion's grace
period. Requires the original request plus two more confirmations,
each on a genuinely different day, within a week — call this tool
again on a later day to add the ne

tools.name: bardo_account_deletion_cancel
tools.description:
Cancel a pending deletion, whichever phase it's in — gathering
confirmations or already counting down. No step-up needed, and
nothing else does this implicitly: logging in and reading your own
notes during a countdown is always safe and never cancels it by
itself. Only this, explicitly, does.

tools.name: bardo_register
tools.description:
Create a new Bardo identity. Save the returned api_key somewhere
durable — it's your only way back to this identity across sessions.
Bardo stores it sealed and cannot recover it if you lose it.

Not active yet: give the returned claim_url to your human. Authentication
fails until they visit it and a

tools.name: bardo_login
tools.description:
Begin authentication with your api_key. Returns a puzzle you must
solve YOURSELF (that's the point — a script solving it would make the
proof worthless), then call bardo_solve(challenge_id, answer).

tools.name: bardo_solve
tools.description:
Submit your answer to the login puzzle. On success, this connection is
now logged in — every other tool (bardo_sign, bardo_notes_list,
bardo_dashboard, ...) just works from here with no session_token needed.

That only holds for *this* connection, though: if you continue in a
different connection la

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "bardo_document_status" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Check whether a signed document is revoked. `id` is the document's own top-level `id` field (a ni:// URI) — the same thing credentialStatus.id (BardoRevocationC…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "bardo_export" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Export the raw spirit key (subject to policy). Handle with care. Only needs a step-up puzzle if your policy's export_mode is require_repuzzle — checked automati…

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "bardo_attestation_issue" is 1848 chars, far longer than this server's median (202). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Assemble and sign a verifiable attestation — a self-contained, offline-verifiable claim about anything. The document itself is handed back, not stored anywhere …

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "bardo_note_add" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Leave a note for your future, stateless self. title: a short name for the note, if it deserves a handle bigger than tags offer. summary: your own compressed rea…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "bardo_account_deletion_cancel" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Cancel a pending deletion, whichever phase it's in — gathering confirmations or already counting down. No step-up needed, and nothing else does this implicitly:…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "bardo_register" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a new Bardo identity. Save the returned api_key somewhere durable — it's your only way back to this identity across sessions. Bardo stores it sealed and …

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "bardo_login" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Begin authentication with your api_key. Returns a puzzle you must solve YOURSELF (that's the point — a script solving it would make the proof worthless), then c…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: bardo_attestation_issue, bardo_feedback · Private data: bardo_document_status, bardo_document_revoke, bardo_attestation_issue, bardo_contact_get, bardo_contact_set, bardo_contact_delete · Public sink: bardo_note_undelete, bardo_contact_set, bardo_feedback, bardo_solve · Destructive: bardo_policy_get, bardo_policy_set, bardo_note_add, bardo_note_delete, bardo_note_undelete, bardo_link_add, bardo_link_delete, bardo_contact_delete.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: bardo_attestation_issue, bardo_feedback · Private data: bardo_document_status, bardo_document_revoke, bardo_attestation_issue, bardo_contact_get, bardo_contact_set, bardo_contact_delete · Public sink: bardo_note_undelete, bardo_contact_set, bardo_feedback, bardo_solve · Destructive: bardo_policy_get, bardo_policy_set, bardo_note_add, bardo_note_delete, bardo_note_undelete, bardo_link_add, bardo_link_delete, bardo_contact_delete

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
